name: Nairobi National Park
description: Only national park in the world bordering a capital city. Home to lions rhinos leopards and over 400 bird species with Nairobi skyline as backdrop
tips: Go early morning for best wildlife sightings. Free entry for children under 3
combined_text: Attraction: Nairobi National Park. Location: Nairobi, Kenya. Type: National Park. Description: Only national park in the world bordering a capital city. Home to lions rhinos leopards and over 400 bird species with Nairobi skyline as backdrop. Price: 20-50 USD. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Go early morning for best wildlife sightings. Free entry for children under 3.

name: Karura Forest
description: Urban forest in Nairobi with walking and cycling trails waterfalls and caves. Perfect for morning runs and picnics
tips: Bring a picnic. Cycling bikes available for hire at the gate
combined_text: Attraction: Karura Forest. Location: Nairobi, Kenya. Type: Nature Park. Description: Urban forest in Nairobi with walking and cycling trails waterfalls and caves. Perfect for morning runs and picnics. Price: 5-10 USD. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Bring a picnic. Cycling bikes available for hire at the gate.

name: David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust
description: Famous elephant orphanage where rescued baby elephants are raised. Daily feeding at 11am is a highlight
tips: Book online in advance. Arrive by 10:45am to get a good spot
combined_text: Attraction: David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust. Location: Nairobi, Kenya. Type: Wildlife Sanctuary. Description: Famous elephant orphanage where rescued baby elephants are raised. Daily feeding at 11am is a highlight. Price: 15 USD. Opening hours: 11am-12pm daily. Tips: Book online in advance. Arrive by 10:45am to get a good spot.

name: Giraffe Centre
description: Feed Rothschild giraffes up close at this conservation centre in Karen. Great for families and children
tips: Buy giraffe pellets at the entrance. Best visited in the morning before crowds
combined_text: Attraction: Giraffe Centre. Location: Nairobi, Kenya. Type: Wildlife Sanctuary. Description: Feed Rothschild giraffes up close at this conservation centre in Karen. Great for families and children. Price: 10-15 USD. Opening hours: 9am-5pm daily. Tips: Buy giraffe pellets at the entrance. Best visited in the morning before crowds.

name: Nairobi National Museum
description: Kenya's premier museum with natural history human evolution exhibits and contemporary art gallery
tips: Allow at least 2 hours. The Snake Park next door is included in the ticket
combined_text: Attraction: Nairobi National Museum. Location: Nairobi, Kenya. Type: Museum. Description: Kenya's premier museum with natural history human evolution exhibits and contemporary art gallery. Price: 10-15 USD. Opening hours: 8:30am-5:30pm daily. Tips: Allow at least 2 hours. The Snake Park next door is included in the ticket.

name: Hell's Gate National Park
description: Unique park where you can cycle and walk among zebras and giraffes. Features dramatic gorges hot springs and towering cliffs
tips: Hire a bicycle at the gate. Fischer's Tower rock climbing is popular. Bring water
combined_text: Attraction: Hell's Gate National Park. Location: Naivasha, Kenya. Type: National Park. Description: Unique park where you can cycle and walk among zebras and giraffes. Features dramatic gorges hot springs and towering cliffs. Price: 15-30 USD. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Hire a bicycle at the gate. Fischer's Tower rock climbing is popular. Bring water.

name: Crescent Island
description: Private island sanctuary on Lake Naivasha where you walk among zebras wildebeest and giraffes with no predators
tips: Reached by boat from Hippo Point. Bring sunscreen as there is no shade
combined_text: Attraction: Crescent Island. Location: Naivasha, Kenya. Type: Wildlife Sanctuary. Description: Private island sanctuary on Lake Naivasha where you walk among zebras wildebeest and giraffes with no predators. Price: 15-25 USD. Opening hours: 7am-6pm daily. Tips: Reached by boat from Hippo Point. Bring sunscreen as there is no shade.

name: Lake Nakuru National Park
description: Famous for flamingos rhinos leopards and lions. One of Africa's most rewarding parks in terms of density of wildlife
tips: Best flamingo viewing is at the southern end of the lake. Rhinos often seen near Makalia Falls
combined_text: Attraction: Lake Nakuru National Park. Location: Nakuru, Kenya. Type: National Park. Description: Famous for flamingos rhinos leopards and lions. One of Africa's most rewarding parks in terms of density of wildlife. Price: 25-50 USD. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Best flamingo viewing is at the southern end of the lake. Rhinos often seen near Makalia Falls.

name: Maasai Mara National Reserve
description: Kenya's most famous game reserve. Home to the Great Migration July to October when over 2 million wildebeest cross the Mara River
tips: Book accommodation 6 months in advance for migration season. Hot air balloon safaris are unforgettable
combined_text: Attraction: Maasai Mara National Reserve. Location: Maasai Mara, Kenya. Type: Game Reserve. Description: Kenya's most famous game reserve. Home to the Great Migration July to October when over 2 million wildebeest cross the Mara River. Price: 80-100 USD. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Book accommodation 6 months in advance for migration season. Hot air balloon safaris are unforgettable.

name: Amboseli National Park
description: Best place in Africa to see elephants up close with Mount Kilimanjaro as backdrop. Over 1600 elephants roam the park
tips: Observation Hill gives panoramic views at sunset. Dust can be heavy so bring a buff or scarf
combined_text: Attraction: Amboseli National Park. Location: Amboseli, Kenya. Type: National Park. Description: Best place in Africa to see elephants up close with Mount Kilimanjaro as backdrop. Over 1600 elephants roam the park. Price: 50-80 USD. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Observation Hill gives panoramic views at sunset. Dust can be heavy so bring a buff or scarf.

name: Fort Jesus
description: 16th century Portuguese fort and UNESCO World Heritage Site with fascinating history of coastal Kenya
tips: Hire a guide at the entrance for much richer experience. Allow 2 hours
combined_text: Attraction: Fort Jesus. Location: Mombasa, Kenya. Type: Historical Site. Description: 16th century Portuguese fort and UNESCO World Heritage Site with fascinating history of coastal Kenya. Price: 1200 KES. Opening hours: 8am-6pm daily. Tips: Hire a guide at the entrance for much richer experience. Allow 2 hours.

name: Diani Beach
description: Consistently ranked among Africa's best beaches. 17km of white sand and turquoise water with excellent snorkeling and water sports
tips: High tide covers most of the beach. Low tide is best for walking. Beware of strong currents during long rains
combined_text: Attraction: Diani Beach. Location: Diani, Kenya. Type: Beach. Description: Consistently ranked among Africa's best beaches. 17km of white sand and turquoise water with excellent snorkeling and water sports. Price: Free. Opening hours: Open always. Tips: High tide covers most of the beach. Low tide is best for walking. Beware of strong currents during long rains.

name: Watamu Marine National Park
description: World class snorkeling and diving with turtles whale sharks and coral gardens. One of Kenya's most biodiverse marine areas
tips: Hire snorkeling gear at the park gate. Turtle nesting season November to March is magical
combined_text: Attraction: Watamu Marine National Park. Location: Watamu, Kenya. Type: Marine Park. Description: World class snorkeling and diving with turtles whale sharks and coral gardens. One of Kenya's most biodiverse marine areas. Price: 20-30 USD. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Hire snorkeling gear at the park gate. Turtle nesting season November to March is magical.

name: Arabuko Sokoke Forest
description: Largest remaining coastal forest in East Africa. Home to rare birds and Clarke's weaver. Excellent for birding and nature walks
tips: Hire a guide from the forest station for best bird sightings. Go early morning
combined_text: Attraction: Arabuko Sokoke Forest. Location: Watamu, Kenya. Type: Forest Reserve. Description: Largest remaining coastal forest in East Africa. Home to rare birds and Clarke's weaver. Excellent for birding and nature walks. Price: 10-15 USD. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Hire a guide from the forest station for best bird sightings. Go early morning.

name: Lamu Old Town
description: Best preserved Swahili settlement in East Africa and UNESCO World Heritage Site. No cars only donkeys and boats
tips: Wear modest clothing. Hire a local guide for cultural insights. Bargain respectfully in the markets
combined_text: Attraction: Lamu Old Town. Location: Lamu, Kenya. Type: UNESCO Heritage Site. Description: Best preserved Swahili settlement in East Africa and UNESCO World Heritage Site. No cars only donkeys and boats. Price: Free to walk. Opening hours: Open always. Tips: Wear modest clothing. Hire a local guide for cultural insights. Bargain respectfully in the markets.

name: Jozani Forest
description: Only national park in Zanzibar. Home to the endangered red colobus monkey found nowhere else on earth
tips: Visit in the morning when monkeys are most active. Guided walks last about 1 hour
combined_text: Attraction: Jozani Forest. Location: Zanzibar, Tanzania. Type: Forest Reserve. Description: Only national park in Zanzibar. Home to the endangered red colobus monkey found nowhere else on earth. Price: 10-15 USD. Opening hours: 7:30am-5pm daily. Tips: Visit in the morning when monkeys are most active. Guided walks last about 1 hour.

name: Nungwi Beach
description: Northernmost beach in Zanzibar known for its stunning sunsets dhow building village and lively beach bars
tips: Visit the dhow building village nearby. Sunset cocktails at beach bars are a must
combined_text: Attraction: Nungwi Beach. Location: Nungwi, Tanzania. Type: Beach. Description: Northernmost beach in Zanzibar known for its stunning sunsets dhow building village and lively beach bars. Price: Free. Opening hours: Open always. Tips: Visit the dhow building village nearby. Sunset cocktails at beach bars are a must.

name: Stone Town
description: Historic heart of Zanzibar City with winding alleys carved doors spice markets and rich Swahili Arab Indian history
tips: Join a spice tour for a half day experience. Evening food tour at Forodhani Gardens is unmissable
combined_text: Attraction: Stone Town. Location: Stone Town, Tanzania. Type: Cultural Site. Description: Historic heart of Zanzibar City with winding alleys carved doors spice markets and rich Swahili Arab Indian history. Price: Free to walk. Opening hours: Open always. Tips: Join a spice tour for a half day experience. Evening food tour at Forodhani Gardens is unmissable.

name: Murchison Falls
description: World's most powerful waterfall where the entire Nile forces through a 7 metre gap. Spectacular boat cruise from below
tips: Boat cruise to the base of the falls is the highlight. Game drives along the Nile are excellent
combined_text: Attraction: Murchison Falls. Location: Murchison Falls, Uganda. Type: Natural Wonder. Description: World's most powerful waterfall where the entire Nile forces through a 7 metre gap. Spectacular boat cruise from below. Price: 40-50 USD. Opening hours: 6am-7pm daily. Tips: Boat cruise to the base of the falls is the highlight. Game drives along the Nile are excellent.

name: Bwindi Impenetrable Forest
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site and home to almost half the world's remaining mountain gorillas. Gorilla trekking is life changing
tips: Book gorilla trekking permits months in advance through Uganda Wildlife Authority. Fitness required for the trek
combined_text: Attraction: Bwindi Impenetrable Forest. Location: Bwindi, Uganda. Type: National Park. Description: UNESCO World Heritage Site and home to almost half the world's remaining mountain gorillas. Gorilla trekking is life changing. Price: 700 USD permit. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Book gorilla trekking permits months in advance through Uganda Wildlife Authority. Fitness required for the trek.

name: Arusha National Park
description: Underrated park near Arusha with Mount Meru flamingos colobus monkeys and stunning montane forest
tips: Canoeing on the alkaline lakes is a unique experience. Mount Meru climb starts here
combined_text: Attraction: Arusha National Park. Location: Arusha, Tanzania. Type: National Park. Description: Underrated park near Arusha with Mount Meru flamingos colobus monkeys and stunning montane forest. Price: 50 USD. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Canoeing on the alkaline lakes is a unique experience. Mount Meru climb starts here.

name: Serengeti National Park
description: Africa's most famous national park covering 15000 square kilometres with the greatest concentration of wildlife on earth
tips: Best combined with Ngorongoro Crater. The annual wildebeest migration mirrors the Maasai Mara crossing
combined_text: Attraction: Serengeti National Park. Location: Arusha, Tanzania. Type: National Park. Description: Africa's most famous national park covering 15000 square kilometres with the greatest concentration of wildlife on earth. Price: 70 USD. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Best combined with Ngorongoro Crater. The annual wildebeest migration mirrors the Maasai Mara crossing.

name: Ngorongoro Crater
description: World's largest intact volcanic caldera and natural enclosure for over 25000 animals including black rhino lions and flamingos
tips: Download offline maps as network is poor inside. Picnic lunch inside the crater is popular
combined_text: Attraction: Ngorongoro Crater. Location: Arusha, Tanzania. Type: Natural Wonder. Description: World's largest intact volcanic caldera and natural enclosure for over 25000 animals including black rhino lions and flamingos. Price: 60 USD conservation fee. Opening hours: 6am-6pm daily. Tips: Download offline maps as network is poor inside. Picnic lunch inside the crater is popular.

name: Kasabi Tombs
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site and burial ground of Buganda kings. Sacred and culturally significant site in Kampala
tips: Dress modestly and remove shoes before entering. Hire a guide for cultural context
combined_text: Attraction: Kasabi Tombs. Location: Kampala, Uganda. Type: UNESCO Heritage Site. Description: UNESCO World Heritage Site and burial ground of Buganda kings. Sacred and culturally significant site in Kampala. Price: 10 USD. Opening hours: 9am-6pm daily. Tips: Dress modestly and remove shoes before entering. Hire a guide for cultural context.

name: Lake Victoria
description: Africa's largest lake and the world's second largest freshwater lake. Sunset boat rides fishing and Rusinga Island are highlights
tips: Hippo Point in Kisumu is the best sunset spot. Do not swim due to bilharzia risk
combined_text: Attraction: Lake Victoria. Location: Kisumu, Kenya. Type: Lake. Description: Africa's largest lake and the world's second largest freshwater lake. Sunset boat rides fishing and Rusinga Island are highlights. Price: Free. Opening hours: Open always. Tips: Hippo Point in Kisumu is the best sunset spot. Do not swim due to bilharzia risk.
